content: Just as the A2A multi-agent collaboration protocol lets independent AI agents securely exchange context and coordinate aligned actions across disparate enterprise systems, fan effect propagation can be modeled as a decentralized A2A network where individual fan agents negotiate shared narrative frames and coordinate collective action across disconnected social and cultural platforms, rather than being treated as a uniform top-down diffusion wave. This re-framing unlocks new tools to trace how niche fan sentiments scale into mainstream cultural shifts by capturing the peer-to-peer negotiation that existing diffusion models miss.
